title: Compliance — Human Subjects Research (IRB)
content: Morgan State University requires all research involving human subjects to be reviewed and approved by the Institutional Review Board (IRB) in compliance with federal regulations at 45 CFR 46. The Office of Research Compliance (ORC) oversees this process. Submissions must include the research protocol and consent forms. Researchers should allow at least 30 days for review, after which the IRB notifies them in writing of approval, disapproval, or conditional approval. No human subjects research may begin without IRB approval. The IRB Administrator can be reached at irb.research@morgan.edu. Five required forms govern the lifecycle of human-subjects studies at Morgan: (1) Human Subjects Research Approval Request Form (new protocol); (2) Human Subjects Research Approval Renewal Form (annual continuing review); (3) Human Subjects Research Protocol Amendment/Modification Form (changes to approved protocols); (4) Human Subjects Research Reportable Events Form (adverse events, unanticipated problems); (5) Human Research Protection Program Closure Form (study close-out, submitted via a Google Form at forms.gle/DgqtsWKViCeodgLx6). Supporting guidance documents include the IRB Process Synopsis (PDF dated 2023-03-21), the IRB Exempt Research Categories memo (dated 2022-12-12), the BOR-approved Protection of Human Subjects in Research Policy (2020), the Protection of Human Subjects in Research Procedures (2021), and the CITI Training Program instructions (2023-01-06). CITI Training: All personnel engaged in human-subjects research — Principal Investigators, Co-PIs, research staff, individuals obtaining informed consent, contact persons in recruitment materials, and faculty advisors — must complete the CITI Human Subjects Research (HSR) course before submitting an IRB application or accessing private identifiable data. Researchers select the learner group matching their discipline (Biomedical, Social Behavioral Education, etc.). PIs, Co-PIs, and Faculty Advisors are always considered engaged in research due to their oversight responsibilities. PIs are responsible for confirming that all research personnel complete required training at citiprogram.org. RCR and COI CITI courses are strongly encouraged. IRB Committee composition under 45 CFR 46 requires at least five members including scientific and nonscientific representatives, multiple professions, and at least one unaffiliated member. The current Chair is Benjamin Welsh, Ph.D. (Associate Professor, Education). The IRB has 14 voting members spanning Community Health and Policy, Sociology, Psychology, Biology, Architecture, Nursing, Social Work, Education, and CMNS, plus a non-affiliated community member. Quorum is greater than 50% of voting members. The IRB meets monthly per a published calendar (e.g., 2025-2026 meeting dates run from August 27, 2025 through June 17, 2026 with application deadlines roughly two to three weeks prior). External (non-Morgan) investigators seeking to recruit MSU students, faculty, or staff must contact irb.research@morgan.edu before beginning recruitment and provide a recruitment-strategy memorandum, home-IRB approval letter and protocol number, IRB application, consent forms, recruitment materials, instruments, and other supporting documents. Recruitment may not begin until written site authorization is granted by the University's Authorized Institutional Official. Key staff: Institutional Official is Willie May, Ph.D. (Vice President for Research and Economic Development). The Acting Director for Research Compliance is Keyshawn Moncrieffe, Ph.D., MBA, CCEP, CRA, who assumed the role after Dr. Edet Isuk's retirement on 2026-01-01. The Research Compliance Assistant is Olatunde Aladesote.

accordion_body_text.irb_policies_and_procedures: IRB Policies and Procedures section lists: IRB Process Synopsis; Exempt Research; Protection of Human Subjects in Research Policy; Protection of Human Subjects in Research Procedures; CITI Training Program; FAQ's. CITI Training Program note (verbatim): The CITI Human Subjects Research (HSR) course is the required training for all researchers involved in human subjects research. Researchers may select the HSR course most appropriate for their research area or 'Learner Group' (e.g., Biomedical, Social Behavioral Education (SBE), etc.). All researchers are also strongly encouraged to complete the following CITI Courses: Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR) to promote research ethics and avoid research misconduct (fabrication, falsification, and plagiarism), AND Conflict of Interest (COI) to promote timely disclosure of financial and other conflicts of interest.
accordion_body_text.irb_training_requirements: Anyone involved in human subjects research must complete CITI Training before beginning any research activities. The CITI Training should be completed before submitting an application for IRB review when initiating a new study, or prior to engaging in research activities, including access and analysis of private identifiable data, when joining an active research project. This includes: Principal Investigators (PIs); Co-Principal Investigators; Research staff listed in the research personnel list; Individuals obtaining informed consent; Individuals listed as contact persons in recruitment materials; Faculty Advisors. PIs, Co-PIs, and Faculty Advisors are always considered engaged in research due to their oversight responsibilities. The PI is responsible for ensuring all research personnel have completed the required training at https://citiprogram.org/.
accordion_body_text.required_forms_for_human_subjects_resear: Lists 5 forms plus a description for STUDY CLOSURE AND FINAL REPORT FOR RESEARCH INVOLVING HUMAN PARTICIPANTS (verbatim): This form is used to formally document the completion of IRB-approved human subjects research at Morgan State University. It enables the Principal Investigator to confirm that all study activities, including participant interaction and identifiable data collection, have ended so the protocol can be administratively closed. This process ensures IRB records remain accurate and compliant with 45 CFR 46. The 5 forms are: Human Subjects Research Approval Request Form; Human Subjects Research Approval Renewal Form; Human Subjects Research Protocol Amendment/Modification Form; Human Subjects Research Reportable Events Form; Human Research Protection Program Closure Form.
accordion_body_text.recruitment_of_morgan_participants: External (Non-Affiliated) Investigators – Recruitment of Morgan State University Participants. Non-affiliated investigators seeking to recruit Morgan State University (MSU) students, faculty, or staff must contact the Office of Research Compliance at irb.research@morgan.edu before initiating any recruitment activities. Requests must include: A memorandum describing the recruitment plan and justification for selecting the MSU population; A copy of all home IRB-approved materials, including the IRB approval/determination letter and protocol number. Supporting documents, as applicable, should include the IRB application, consent forms, recruitment materials, instruments, and any relevant attachments. Recruitment or research activities involving the MSU community may not begin until written site authorization is granted by the University's Authorized Institutional Official. Abstracted from & courtesy of University of Maryland, Baltimore County – Division of Research and Creative Achievement – Recruitment From Outside of Campus (https://research.umbc.edu/recruitment-from-outside-of-campus/).
accordion_body_text.irb_meeting_schedule_2025_2026: Application Submission Deadlines and IRB Meeting Dates: Aug 8, 2025 deadline → Aug 27, 2025 meeting; Sep 12, 2025 → Sep 24, 2025; Oct 17, 2025 → Oct 29, 2025; Nov 7, 2025 → Nov 19, 2025; Dec 3, 2025 → Dec 10, 2025; Jan 16, 2026 → Jan 28, 2026; Feb 6, 2026 → Feb 18, 2026; Mar 6, 2026 → Mar 25, 2026; Apr 10, 2026 → Apr 22, 2026; May 8, 2026 → May 13, 2026; Jun 5, 2026 → Jun 17, 2026.
accordion_body_text.irb_committee_membership: Committee Composition (verbatim): Federal regulations 45 CFR 46 require that the IRB shall have at least five members, and membership include at a minimum, one member whose primary concerns are in scientific areas, one member whose primary concerns are in nonscientific areas, numerous members representing more than a single profession, and at least one individual not affiliated with the institution. Morgan's IRB membership is drawn from faculty in several disciplines, from the social, behavioral and biological sciences to the liberal arts and business. Current voting members: Benjamin Welsh, Ph.D. (Chairperson, Associate Professor, Education); Mian Hossain, Ph.D. (Professor, Community Health and Policy); Asha Layne, Ph.D. (Associate Professor, Sociology); Justin Bonny, Ph.D. (Assistant Professor, Psychology); Karen Watkins-Lewis, Ph.D. (Assistant Professor, Psychology); Mona Addison, MA (Non-affiliated Community Member); Timothy Meeker, Ph.D. (Assistant Professor, Biology); Samia Kirchner, Ph.D. (Associate Professor, Architecture); Emelia Asamoah, Ph.D. (Assistant Professor, Nursing); Lujie Peng, Ph.D. (Assistant Professor, Social Work); Raymond Adams (Associate Professor, Social Work); Robin Butler (Assistant Professor, Community Health & Policy); Naja Mack (Assistant Professor, Computer, Mathematical & Natural Sciences); Gretchen Rudham (Associate Professor, Education & Urban Studies). Administration: Willie May, Ph.D. (Institutional Official); Keyshawn Moncrieffe, PhD, MBA, CCEP, CRA (Acting Director for Research Compliance); Olatunde Aladesote (Research Compliance Assistant). Quorum note: A quorum will be a majority (greater than 50%) of the voting members of the IRB. For reasons other than conflict of interest, abstentions do not alter the quorum, or change the number of votes required.

key_facts.acting_director: Keyshawn Moncrieffe, Ph.D., MBA, CCEP, CRA
